steps.title: Find the side length of square Q
steps.content:
Square P has side length $x$, giving it a perimeter of $4x$ inches. Square Q's perimeter is 176 inches greater: $4x + 176$. The side length of Q is this perimeter divided by 4:
$$\text{Side of Q} = \frac{4x + 176}{4} = x + 44$$

steps.title: Find the area function
steps.content:
The area of a square equals the side length squared:
$$f(x) = (x + 44)^2$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
